name: Whalen v. United States
summary: Held that the Double Jeopardy Clause bars cumulative punishments for greater and lesser-included offenses absent a clear indication that the legislature intended to authorize them.
significance: Established that the Blockburger presumption requires 'special authorization' from Congress to impose cumulative punishments for the same offense. Petitioner relies on its reasoning about lesser-included offenses in the rape/felony-murder context.
